pullRequest.title: [DOM-75573] Fix bug in endpoints-apps interaction PR

nodes.body:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_

**Assert the actual fallback ref, not just the resolved SHA.**

Both new fallback tests use `any[Reference]`, so they still pass even if the wrong fallback source is used. Please verify the exact `Reference` passed to `gitUriTools.resolveRefToCommitId`.


<details>
<summary>Suggested test strengthening</summary>

```diff
-import org.mockito.ArgumentMatchers.any
+import org.mockito.ArgumentMatchers.{any, eq => eqTo}
@@
   it should "fall back to project default branch when mainRepoGitRef is None for git-based project" in new Fixture {
     val projectDefaultBranch = ReferenceDTO("branches", Some("develop"))
     val projectWithDefaultBranch = TestUtils.makeProject(mainRepository = Some(mainRepo)).copy(projectDefaultBranch = Some(projectDefaultBranch))
+    val expectedRef = ReferenceDTO.get(projectDefaultBranch.`type`, projectDefaultBranch.value)
     val resolvedSha = "b" * 40
@@
     val result = resolver.resolveGitRef(principal, projectId, None)

     result shouldBe Some(resolvedSha)
+    verify(gitUriTools).resolveRefToCommitId(
+      any[String],
+      any[RepositoryCredentials],
+      eqTo(expectedRef),
+    )(any[DominoContext])
   }
@@
   it should "fall back to repository default ref when mainRepoGitRef is None and no project default branch" in new Fixture {
+    val expectedRef = ReferenceDTO.get(mainRepo.defaultRef.`type`, mainRepo.defaultRef.value)
     val resolvedSha = "c" * 40
@@
     val result = resolver.resolveGitRef(principal, projectId, None)

     result shouldBe Some(resolvedSha)
+    verify(gitUriTools).resolveRefToCommitId(
+      any[String],
+      any[RepositoryCredentials],
+      eqTo(expectedRef),
+    )(any[DominoContext])
   }
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In
`@apps/monolith-adapter/src/test/scala/domino/apps/services/DefaultCommitResolverSpec.scala`
around lines 52 - 79, The tests for resolveGitRef are too permissive because
they stub gitUriTools.resolveRefToCommitId with
any[domino.git.references.Reference], so they don't verify the actual fallback
Reference used; update the two tests using
projectDefaultBranch/projectWithDefaultBranch and gitProject to capture or
verify the Reference argument passed into gitUriTools.resolveRefToCommitId
(e.g., with an ArgumentCaptor or eq(...) matcher) and assert it equals the
expected fallback Reference (projectDefaultBranch for projectWithDefaultBranch
case, and the repository default Reference for gitProject) while keeping the
rest of the stubbing for resolvedSha the same.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:poseidon:hawk -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated reply by CodeRabbit -->

✅ Confirmed as addressed by @adrianrsy

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

`@adrianrsy`, confirmed! The file now includes the `verify` calls with exact `eqTo` matchers — `eqTo(GitBranchReference("develop"))` for the project default branch fallback and `eqTo(GitHeadReference)` for the repository default ref fallback — ensuring the correct reference is actually exercised in each test. Looks great! 🎉

<!-- <review_comment_addressed> -->

---

<!-- This is an auto-generated reply by CodeRabbit -->
this assumes defaultRef is always present on a repo and if not, this would throw. Would that be a problem here?
defaultRef is a required field and should always be present. Should be defaulting to the head ref.
